verdict.note: Real browser (Playwright): same 'login' remark on two readings of one scenario. Reading B (12s, re-measure) resolves login by step-name to its LIVE 6.0s (chip re-derived ▶0:06, seeked video.currentTime=6.0), NOT the frozen 0:02; 'logout' (absent from B's timeline) degrades to a non-seekable ▶0:05 ⚠ chip. Reading A (6s) resolves login to 2.0s (▶0:02) and logout stays seekable ▶0:05. m:ss derived per-clip, survives re-measure, never silently wrong.

Verified through the real code path (pickLoopIn, the fallback decision notifyOriginator uses): filer online -> filer; filer OFFLINE -> falls through to node governing session; whole chain offline -> none (silent, teeth still surface it); a link already reached by an explicit @-target -> stop (no double-delivery); filer==replier pruned -> governing session reached. mentions.test.ts 14/14 pass. Notification resolves nothing (resolve stays a deliberate spex resolve); never spawns (only @new spawns).

Verified end-to-end via the real worktree CLI on a scratch git repo: remarked scenario 'old-name', then renamed it in yatsu.md. BEFORE rename (declared+unmeasured) -> yatsu-missing, NOT dangling. AFTER rename -> 'spex yatsu scan' prints a yatsu-dangling line + '1 dangling' in the summary; evalTimeline.dangling carries the orphaned track with its remark ref (eval-mynode-old-name#rs0le), kept OUT of readings/latestPerScenario (no scoreboard contamination). Control scenario 'keep' stays fresh (0 stale) -> the orphan ages nothing. 'spex retract <ref>' on the dangling remark works -> orphan clears. Node-level DanglingTrack row (struck-through) rendered in NodeView.
rig with real rendezvous sockets: filer→gov→silent chain, stop-at-first-link, no double-delivery on explicit @, resolved bit untouched, zero spawns
